actor_relation_claims.quote_text: On March 28, 2013, sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch to provide financial advisory services.
a representative from the law firm of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (“Gibson Dunn”), which had been engaged in October 2012 as the company’s outside corporate counsel

event_claims.description: The board determined it would undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: As it evaluated the advisability and potential long-term impacts of the cost reductions, our board of directors determined that it would be beneficial to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives, including exploring a potential sale of sTec.

event_claims.description: The board authorized management to contact potential financial advisors for the strategic alternatives review, including a potential sale.
event_claims.quote_text: Consequently, in mid-November, 2012, our board of directors authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to explore their interest in representing the company in its exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the company.

event_claims.description: The board established a special committee to review strategic alternatives, including a possible sale.
event_claims.quote_text: During the meeting on February 13, our board of directors established a special committee of the board, initially composed of Dr. Daly, and Messrs. Colpitts and Witte, and subsequently expanded to four members on March 26, 2013.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec at the direction of the special committee.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, as directed by the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec.

event_claims.description: sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to exploring a potential sale.
event_claims.quote_text: In total sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 4, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E, a participant in the storage industry

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 10, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
event_claims.quote_text:
on April 11, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F, another

27
<!-- PAGE 36 -->

participant in the storage industry

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 17, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G, a participant in the storage industry.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on April 17, 2013, sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.

event_claims.description: The special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company C after Company C indicated interest only in limited, select assets.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 15, 2013, Company C indicated that it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of the company, and as a result, the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company C.

event_claims.description: Company F declined to schedule a management presentation and had no further communications regarding a possible transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: on April 24, Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and Company F had no further communications with BofA Merrill Lynch or sTec regarding a possible transaction.

event_claims.description: The special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E after Company E indicated interest only in limited, select assets.
event_claims.quote_text: Shortly thereafter, Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and as a result the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E.

event_claims.description: sTec management held a meeting with WDC representatives about a possible transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 18, 2013, members of our management held a meeting with representatives of WDC, at which our management provided a presentation on sTec, and the attendees discussed matters related to a possible transaction between the two companies.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 23, 2013, at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on April 26, 2013, at the direction of our chairman on behalf of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company D verbally indicated interest in pursuing a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On the call, Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share and detailed their additional diligence requirements for submitting a written indication of interest.

event_claims.actor_label: WDC, Company H, and Company D
event_claims.description: WDC, Company H, and Company D submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: Of these, three prospective acquirers (WDC, Company H, a participant in the storage industry, and Company D) submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec

participation_count_claims.quote_text: In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.
In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.
In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.
Of these, three prospective acquirers (WDC, Company H, a participant in the storage industry, and Company D) submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec
six potential strategic acquirers expressed an interest in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec
nine prospective acquirers, including the financial sponsor and Company A, indicated they were not interested in a potential transaction.
In total sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.
On April 23, 2013, at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.
